mnemonics: RICE - Read, Interpret, Create, Execute - the four main steps of the shell cycle
keyPoints: The shell reads a command from standard input
The shell interprets and parses the command, expanding variables and wildcards
The shell creates a new process using fork() system call
The shell executes the command using exec() system call in the child process
The parent process waits for the child to complete using wait()
commonMistakes: Forgetting that variable expansion happens before command execution
Confusing the roles of fork() and exec() - fork creates the process, exec loads the program
Assuming the shell executes commands directly rather than creating child processes
Not understanding that wildcard expansion (*.txt) is done by the shell, not the program
